data.description:
## Why
Adjudication of 68 would-block/near-block plan-review findings showed the gate UNDER-blocks: 15/20
findings in the 0.60-0.69 band on blocking criteria were block-worthy with ZERO false-positives, and
six currently-advisory criteria scored 75-100% block-worthy at their would-block slice. The 0.70 bar
strands real blocks and the blocking set omits load-bearing criteria.

## What
Apply the approved per-criterion thresholds/postures in the plan-review routing config:
- Lower `block_threshold` 0.70 -> 0.60 for: COH, E2, F1, G5, G6, T5e.
- Keep T4 @ 0.70 (its band findings were fail-safe migrations).
- Promote to `default_posture: blocking`: G1G2 @0.70, T1 @0.70, T8 @0.70, and E4 @0.75.
- E1, T3, A1 remain advisory (no threshold reaches 100% block-worthy; revisit later).

## Scope
- Edit `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json` and the plan-review unit tests that assert
  per-criterion `block_threshold`/`default_posture`. No engine or aggregation changes here.
- Out of scope: the impact-aggregation redesign (separate child); E1/T3 promotion (deferred).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `criteria_routing.json` matches the table above (per-criterion `block_threshold` + `default_posture`).
- [ ] Plan-review unit tests updated and green; `make lint` (ruff + format) passes.
- [ ] An offline replay confirms the six lowered criteria now block their previously-stranded 0.60-0.69
      band findings, and no criterion outside the approved set changes posture.
data.title: Plan-review threshold + posture recalibration (approved pre-redesign changes)
